description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun with a boxy, utilitarian design reminiscent of the MAC-10. The main body is constructed from metal, featuring a rectangular receiver with a prominent magazine well extending downward. A simple cylindrical barrel protrudes from the front, and a folding metal wire stock is attached to the rear, curving over the top of the receiver. The grip is straight and vertical, with a trigger guard and a visible magazine inserted. The top of the receiver has a basic iron sight system, and the overall finish is matte gray, suggesting a functional, industrial build. The design is minimalistic, with few embellishments, emphasizing practicality and ease of use in close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, boxy submachine gun with a folding wire stock, straight magazine, and matte gray metal finish. Its utilitarian design is suited for close-quarters use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the receiver, folding wire stock, grip, and magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the cylindrical barrel, front sight, and the width of the receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the receiver, folding stock, grip, and magazine from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the receiver, the wire stock, and the alignment of the iron sights.
